reflection:
you made it home. antalya to istanbul to zagreb to ljubljana, and you still pulled six hours plus of sleep, more than you'd been getting all week. that counts, even if your body felt like a wet rag in zagreb airport.

here's the thing i want you to actually hear. that pounding heart on waking, pulse over 90 standing, normal again an hour later. you've logged it in antalya, mid travel, and now back home in your own bed. three different beds, same spike. that's not a place doing it to you. it's your nervous system pulling the morning alarm before you're awake to argue with it. your worry dump said you're scared you're still somehow sick. the numbers push back. whoop has you at 65 percent recovery this morning, 96 percent sleep efficiency. a sick body coming off four flights does not climb back to green that fast. you're depleted, not broken.

and look at yesterday. massage with mitya, ping pong and a walk with matjaz, lunch out, hair with anja. that is not a man falling apart. that's a tired one who still showed up.

one thing today. you flew in running on empty and the morning surge hits harder on an empty tank. get a real breakfast with protein into you within the first hour, before claude and the day swallow you whole. give your system something solid to settle on.

welcome home. be gentle with the guy who just did all that. ❤️
opener: you made it home. depleted, not broken, and the morning spike isn't illness
focus_for_today: real breakfast with protein within an hour of waking, before claude and the day take over
